operating_state: Consolidated revenues grew 11% to $72.3 billion driven by 2.0 million additional people served domestically through Medicare Advantage and Medicaid expansion, commercial growth from acquisitions, and Optum revenue growth from care delivery expansion and managed services. UnitedHealthcare served 50.4 million people globally with Medicare Advantage increasing 14% and Medicaid growing 17% due to states easing redetermination requirements during COVID-19.
cost_structure: Medical care ratio increased to 83.0% from 81.9% due to permanent Health Insurance Tax repeal and COVID-19 related care costs, partially offset by temporary care deferrals. Operating cost ratio improved to 14.8% from 15.6% driven by the tax repeal and efficiency gains despite business mix changes.
material_events: Board increased quarterly dividend to $5.80 annually from $5.00 per share in June 2021. Company repurchased 10 million shares at average price of $376.93 during nine months, with 48 million shares remaining under authorization. Pending Change Healthcare acquisition requires approximately $9 billion capital excluding debt payoff, subject to regulatory approvals.
macro_exposures: COVID-19 pandemic continues creating uncertainty with care patterns potentially exceeding normal baselines as deferred care resumes with temporarily higher acuity. Medicare Advantage 2022 rates increased 4.1% industry-wide, falling short of forward medical cost trends requiring continued cost management through coordinated care models.
forward_outlook: Management expects continued competition in commercial risk markets as industry adapts to reform changes. Government programs anticipated to receive lower rate increases than commercial markets due to budget pressures. Company maintains focus on managing medical cost trends through physician and consumer engagement while expanding coordinated care models.
uncertainty_profile: Ultimate COVID-19 impact remains uncertain depending on pandemic pacing and economic conditions, with potential for increased care demand and higher acuity from deferred treatments. Medical cost reserve estimation faces increased variability due to COVID-19's continued uncertain impact. DOJ legal action regarding risk adjustment submissions represents ongoing regulatory matter requiring resolution.
